things.name: Venetian Walls
things.description: Magnificent 16th-century star-shaped Venetian fortifications encircle the old city; eleven heart-shaped bastions and three gates still define Nicosia's layout

things.name: Cyprus Museum
things.description: The island's premier archaeological museum with a breathtaking collection of Cypriot antiquities spanning 9,000 years from the Neolithic to Byzantine periods

things.name: Green Line
things.description: The UN buffer zone dividing the world's last partitioned capital; cross the Ledra Street checkpoint to visit the Turkish-Cypriot north of this unique city

things.name: Selimiye Mosque (former St. Sophia Cathedral)
things.description: Superb Gothic cathedral from 1326 converted to a mosque after the Ottoman conquest of 1570; the finest Gothic building in the Eastern Mediterranean

things.name: Laiki Geitonia (Old Town)
things.description: Restored traditional neighbourhood within the Venetian walls with craft workshops, tavernas and winding lanes full of Cypriot character

things.name: Leventis Municipal Museum
things.description: Award-winning museum tracing Nicosia's history from antiquity to modern times through rich artefact collections in a beautifully restored building

things.name: Archbishop's Palace
things.description: Byzantine complex housing the Byzantine Museum with an extraordinary collection of icons spanning 900 years; the seat of the Cypriot Orthodox Church

things.name: Ledra Street
things.description: Nicosia's main pedestrian shopping street cutting through the old town to the UN checkpoint, lined with shops, cafes and street performers

things.name: Athalassa National Forest Park
things.description: Large forest park south of Nicosia with lakes, walking trails and a nature reserve; the lung of the capital and popular recreation area for residents

things.name: GSP Stadium
things.description: Home of both APOEL FC and Omonia Nicosia, Cyprus's two most famous clubs and regular UEFA competition participants, with a 22,700 capacity
